Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Digestive • De-bloating • Postpartum recovery

CCF Tea

Cumin, coriander & fennel — the classic Ayurvedic tea that calms digestion and bloat.
Makes 4 cups
Time 10 min
Caffeine none
For you · sip after meals
When to drink After meals, or through the day. Warm and caffeine-free — especially soothing if you feel bloated.

Why this heals you

  • Cumin — traditionally eases gas & sluggish digestion tradition
  • Coriander — cooling; supports gentle detox tradition
  • Fennel — calms bloating; traditionally supports milk supply tradition
  • Caffeine-free — a hydrating daily habit tradition
Ingredients
½ tspCumin seeds
½ tspCoriander seeds
½ tspFennel seeds
4 cupsFiltered water
Method
1
Measure the seeds

½ tsp each of cumin, coriander and fennel seeds.

2
Simmer

Add to 4 cups water. Bring to a boil, then simmer 5 min.

3
Strain & sip

Strain out the seeds. Sip warm through the day.

Just for you, Mum

A gentle after-meal ritual — CCF is the go-to Ayurvedic tea for a tender postpartum gut. Keep a flask warm on the counter.

Good to know
BestWarm, after meals or between them.
BatchDouble it and keep in a flask all day.
Why we say this
Cumin–coriander–fennel is a well-documented Ayurvedic digestive tea; framed as traditional supportive use, not a medical treatment.
Main Meals
Snacks
Drinks
Desserts
Food, not medical advice · non-diagnostic · made for this season of motherhood
